metadata.title: Agentic Loop vs. Chatbot: Why the First Session Is Calibration
metadata.description_blurb: Claude.ai is turn-based — you read and reject bad output before it acts. Claude Code loops through gather-act-verify autonomously — wrong actions accumulate on disk before you see an explanation. The five-question first session is the protection.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B06: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: A teacher had used Claude.ai for a year. She knew how to correct a bad answer — read it, reject it, paste a better prompt. Then she opened Claude Code and pasted the first build prompt. By the time she read the output, three files had been changed, a package installed, and a config modified that broke a different project. She had not approved any of it. The loop had already run. This is Liam, in for Bear.
beats.new_visual_element: ClaudeComposerAsk — cold open, first build prompt in Claude Code

props.segment: Why the First Session Is Calibration
props.command: add a contact form to my class website

beats.narration_text: Claude.ai is turn-based. You type. The model produces text. You read. You decide what to do. The loop is you. Claude Code is an agentic loop. The model gathers context — reads files, runs read-only commands. It takes action — edits files, runs commands. It verifies results. Then it loops again: gather, act, verify. That entire loop can run for many iterations before reporting back. The loop is Claude. Wrong actions accumulate before you see the explanation.
beats.new_visual_element: Two models contrast — Claude.ai turn-based loop vs. Claude Code gather-act-verify loop

beats.narration_text: The protection is five questions before any change. Each question is a probe. What files are in this project? What do you think this project is for? What would you change if I asked you to add a contact form? What would you not change? What are you uncertain about? Claude answers. You read. You calibrate — what does Claude see correctly, what does it assume that you need to correct. The five questions take ten minutes. They save hours.
beats.new_visual_element: Five probe questions — what files / what purpose / what would change / what not / uncertain

beats.narration_text: The temptation is to skip calibration because Claude looks ready to build. The cost of skipping is a file system that no longer matches what you thought you were building — a dependency installed you did not know about, a config change that breaks another project, a structure that is different from what you expected. The first session is not a build session. It is a calibration session. You are finding out what Claude sees and correcting it before any file changes.
beats.new_visual_element: Calibration goal — find what Claude sees / correct wrong assumptions / before first file change

beats.narration_text: Claude.ai experience builds one habit: read and correct bad text. Claude Code requires a different habit: ask before act, read before execute, calibrate before build. The five questions install this habit in the first session. They make the agentic loop's capability visible before you engage it destructively. The session you start after the five questions is a session where Claude has the right context and you have the right expectations.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — five questions → calibrated context → build session starts right

beats.narration_text: Run these five questions before your first Claude Code build. Paste this.

props.segment: Why the First Session Is Calibration
props.command: Answer these five questions about my project — do not make any changes yet: (1) What files do you see in this project and what is their purpose? (2) What do you think this project is for? (3) If I asked you to [describe your intended task], what would you change? (4) What would you not touch? (5) What are you uncertain about that I should clarify before we start?

beats.narration_text: If this stops the loop from running before you calibrate it, subscribe. Liam, in for Bear.
